2604.19593.title: RoLegalGEC: Legal Domain Grammatical Error Detection and Correction Dataset for Romanian
2604.19593.paper_summary: The importance of clear and correct text in legal documents cannot be understated, and, consequently, a grammatical error correction tool meant to assist a professional in the law must have the ability to understand the possible errors in the context of a legal environment, correcting them accordingly, and implicitly needs to be trained in the same environment, using realistic legal data. However, the manually annotated data required by such a process is in short supply for languages such as Romanian, much less for a niche domain. The most common approach is the synthetic generation of parallel data; however, it requires a structured understanding of the Romanian grammar. In this paper, we introduce, to our knowledge, the first Romanian-language parallel dataset for the detection and correction of grammatical errors in the legal domain, RoLegalGEC, which aggregates 350,000 examples of errors in legal passages, along with error annotations. Moreover, we evaluate several neural network models that transform the dataset into a valuable tool for both detecting and correcting grammatical errors, including knowledge-distillation Transformers, sequence tagging architectures for detection, and a variety of pre-trained text-to-text Transformer models for correction. We consider that the set of models, together with the novel RoLegalGEC dataset, will enrich the resource base for further research on Romanian.
2604.19593.paper_summary_zh: 法律文件中清晰且正確的文本的重要性不容小覷，因此，旨在協助法律專業人士的語法錯誤修正工具必須具備理解法律環境中可能出現的錯誤的能力，並相應地進行修正，並且隱含地需要在相同環境中進行訓練，使用真實的法律數據。然而，這一過程所需的手動標註數據在羅馬尼亞語等語言中供應不足，更不用說一個小眾領域了。最常見的方法是合成生成平行數據；然而，這需要對羅馬尼亞語法有結構化的理解。在本文中，我們介紹了據我們所知的第一個羅馬尼亞語平行數據集，用於法律領域的語法錯誤檢測和修正，RoLegalGEC，它匯聚了350,000個法律段落中的錯誤示例及錯誤標註。此外，我們評估了幾種神經網絡模型，將數據集轉化為一個有價值的工具，用於檢測和修正語法錯誤，包括知識蒸餾Transformer、用於檢測的序列標記架構，以及各種預訓練的文本到文本Transformer模型以進行修正。我們認為這組模型，加上新穎的RoLegalGEC數據集，將豐富羅馬尼亞語進一步研究的資源基礎。
2604.19593.author: Mircea Timpuriu et.al.
2604.19593.authors: Mircea Timpuriu, Mihaela-Claudia Cercel, Dumitru-Clementin Cercel
